- summary: 502 means gateway got bad response; 503 means temporarily unavailable. Users on CDN see 502—could be edge, but more often origin can't handle load—comparative …

## Slow DNS Propagation: TTL, Carrier Cache, and Regional Resolver Differences
- slug: dns-propagation-slow
- category: Troubleshooting
- keywords: DNS cache,TTL,propagation,SpeedCE,migration
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/dns-propagation-slow.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/dns-propagation-slow.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/dns-propagation-slow.md
- summary: DNS doesn't change worldwide at once. TTL=86400 means up to 24 hours worst case. SpeedCE every 10 minutes—random fading vs stubborn fixed provinces.

- summary: "Only Xinjiang" or "only Mobile"—averages and success rates don't help. Maps are the language of regional faults. SpeedCE China map is built for this.

- summary: China Mobile users exceed 50%, but many "optimized routes" only help Telecom/Unicom. Skip Mobile map = ignore half your users.

- keywords: single carrier fault,Telecom,Unicom,Mobile,SpeedCE

- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/single-carrier-fault.md
- summary: Split three carriers—one all red shrinks fault domain by 66%. Route issue, CDN per-carrier config, or carrier DNS? Comparative tests point the way.

- summary: Bandwidth, international egress, attack traffic—peak hours are the truth mirror. Sellers show test IP in afternoon; you retest 20:00-22:00 on SpeedCE.

## Mixed Content and HTTPS: Network Green but Browser Still "Not Secure"
- slug: mixed-content-https
- category: Troubleshooting
- keywords: mixed content,HTTPS,frontend security,SpeedCE,SSL
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/mixed-content-https.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/mixed-content-https.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/mixed-content-https.md
- summary: SpeedCE tests site reachability; mixed content is http:// assets on HTTPS pages. Clear division—don't waste time on network layer for browser-only issues.

## CORS Errors vs Network Failure: Two Layers Developers Must Separate
- slug: cors-vs-network-testing
- category: Troubleshooting
- keywords: CORS,cross-origin,API,frontend,SpeedCE
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/cors-vs-network-testing.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/cors-vs-network-testing.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/cors-vs-network-testing.md
- summary: Map all green + browser CORS error—congrats, network works; server headers missing. SpeedCE rules out network, then check Access-Control-Allow-Origin.

## Database Killing the Site: Network Green but Page Timeout—Application Layer Triage
- slug: database-not-network-guide
- category: Troubleshooting
- keywords: database,slow query,502,application layer,SpeedCE
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/database-not-network-guide.html

- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/database-not-network-guide.md
- summary: SpeedCE green + page timeout—network is fine; check MySQL slow queries and connection pool exhaustion. Network first, application second—order matters.

## Stale Cache: CDN/Browser Cache vs Network Layer Comparative Troubleshooting
- slug: cache-poisoning-stale
- category: Troubleshooting
- keywords: CDN cache,browser cache,troubleshooting,SpeedCE
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/cache-poisoning-stale.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/cache-poisoning-stale.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/cache-poisoning-stale.md
- summary: You fixed the server; users still see old page—likely cache. SpeedCE probes live network response; add random query or test API to bypass cache.

## CN2 GT vs CN2 GIA: Speed Test Verification Behind Marketing Claims

- keywords: CN2 GT,CN2 GIA,VPS,route,SpeedCE
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/cn2-gt-vs-gia.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/cn2-gt-vs-gia.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/cn2-gt-vs-gia.md
- summary: Two letters difference, one tier of experience. GT may congest at peak; GIA costs more but stable. Trust three-carrier maps + peak retest, not copy.

## Real vs Fake BGP: Three-Carrier Balance Is the Acceptance Standard
- slug: bgp-line-verification
- category: VPS Routing
- keywords: BGP,VPS,three-carrier,route test,SpeedCE
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/bgp-line-verification.html

- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/bgp-line-verification.md
- summary: True BGP: Telecom, Unicom, Mobile all usable. Fake BGP: Telecom green, Mobile red. SpeedCE three-carrier split is the mirror.

## ICMP Blocked ≠ Bad Route: Reading PING Red with HTTPS Green
- slug: ping-blocked-not-bad
- category: VPS Routing
- keywords: ICMP blocked,VPS,speed test,SpeedCE
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/ping-blocked-not-bad.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/ping-blocked-not-bad.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/ping-blocked-not-bad.md
- summary: Newbies panic on ping timeout. Cloud default ICMP block is normal. Acceptance standard: HTTPS success ≥ 90%.

## Off-Peak vs Peak VPS Testing: Why Good Routes Need Two Tests
- slug: off-peak-vs-peak-vps
- category: VPS Routing
- keywords: peak hour,VPS,route test,SpeedCE
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/off-peak-vs-peak-vps.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/off-peak-vs-peak-vps.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/off-peak-vs-peak-vps.md
- summary: Afternoon test IP looks great; peak hour tells truth. Quality routes must pass both windows.

- summary: Your home ping is one sample on one carrier in one province. Nationwide probe nodes represent real user distribution.

- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/spring-festival-traffic.md
- summary: Holiday mobile usage spikes—pre-festival three-carrier maps on all conversion domains.

## Kubernetes Ingress Failures: Cluster Internal OK, Public Domain Red

- category: Troubleshooting
- keywords: Kubernetes,Ingress,troubleshooting,SpeedCE,public URL

- summary: kubectl works inside; public URL red—Ingress, LB, and cert are separate from pod health. SpeedCE tests the public path.

## Let's Encrypt Rate Limits and Renewal Failures: When HTTPS Suddenly Goes Red Nationwide
- slug: lets-encrypt-rate-limit

- keywords: Let's Encrypt,rate limit,certificate,HTTPS,SpeedCE

- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/lets-encrypt-rate-limit.md
- summary: Cert renewal hit rate limit at 3 AM—HTTPS nationwide red. SpeedCE confirms cert layer; fix ACME and renew.

## SNI Mismatch: Partial HTTPS Failures with Multiple Certs on One IP
- slug: sni-mismatch-error
- category: Troubleshooting
- keywords: SNI,HTTPS,multiple certificates,SpeedCE,TLS
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/sni-mismatch-error.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/sni-mismatch-error.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/sni-mismatch-error.md
- summary: Some nodes red, some green on same domain—classic SNI or wrong default cert. Test HTTPS from multiple regions.

- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/tls-version-too-low.md
- summary: Disabled TLS 1.0 may break legacy clients in specific regions—map pattern differs from cert expiry.

- summary: Site "works" but TTFB huge—compression misconfig or proxy timeout. Network green, user experience red.

- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/affiliate-tracking-domain.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/affiliate-tracking-domain.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/affiliate-tracking-domain.md
- summary: Affiliate click blocked in one province loses commission—probe tracking domains on three carriers.

## A/B Test Traffic Split Domains: Independent Map Acceptance per Experiment Arm
- slug: ab-test-traffic-split
- category: Advanced
- keywords: A/B test,traffic split,domain,SpeedCE,acceptance
- pages: https://freejbgo.github.io/SpeedCE-English-Tech/articles/ab-test-traffic-split.html
- github: https://github.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/blob/main/articles/ab-test-traffic-split.md
- raw: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/freejbgo/SpeedCE-English-Tech/main/articles/ab-test-traffic-split.md
- summary: Experiment subdomain must accept independently—don't assume control map applies to variant.
